rulings.Case Title: GRADY FRAZIER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute Claim
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 05, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Thomas Dee Engineering Co.¿s motion to dismiss for failure to prosecute pursuant to C.C.P. 583.410-583.430 is denied without prejudice as untimely. Defendant¿s proof of service demonstrates that defendant failed to provide proper notice to Mr. Frazier as required by CRC 3.1342(a) and C.C.P. 1005(b). If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GRADY FRAZIER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 05, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant CBS Corp.¿s motion to dismiss for failure to prosecute pursuant to C.C.P. 583.410-583.430 is granted. No opposition filed. Hearing required unless defendant notifies plaintiff of this tentative ruling and he states that he does not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Defendant must bring a proposed order to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ELAINE PERCY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 05, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Defendant Clausen-Patten, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs did not produce admissible evidence that defendant knew or reasonably should have known that the installation, use and/or disturbance of asbestos-containing products or materials could create a safety hazard of secondary exposure for a member of a household of a non-employee. Even if decedent¿s injury was foreseeable, pursuant to Rowland v. Christian (1968) 69 Cal.2d 108 and Campbell v. Ford Motor Company (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th15, public policy dictates against a creation of a duty in the present matter. Plaintiffs have dismissed their strict liability and false representation causes of action and claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BRUCE MAHER VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 05, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3, Defendant Kerr Corp.¿s motion for summary adjudication is denied as to punitive damages and off calendar as to premises owner/contractor liability. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of negating an essential element of plaintiff¿s punitive damages claim. Plaintiff represents that he is dismissing his premises owner/contractor liability cause of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BRUCE MAHER VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 05, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3, Defendant DAP, Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication is off calendar. A dismissal is on file as to plaintiff¿s premises/contractor liability cause of action and claim for punitive damages, which were the only issues defendant sought to have summarily adjudicated. = (503/TLJ)
